import io
import string
import list

fn remove_at(_ xs: List<Int>, _ idx: Int) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = xs.len()
  while i < n
    if i != idx
      match xs.get(i)
      when Some(v)
        result = list.push(result, v)
      when None
        ()
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  "0"
end

let tokens = string.split_whitespace(line1)
mut nums: List<Int> = []
mut ti = 0
while ti < tokens.len()
  match tokens.get(ti)
  when Some(t)
    match string.parse_int(t)
    when Some(n)
      nums = list.push(nums, n)
    when None
      ()
    end
  when None
    ()
  end
  ti = ti + 1
end

let k = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
when Some(n)
  n
when None
  0
end

let take = if k > nums.len()
  nums.len()
else
  k
end

mut output = ""
mut remaining = nums
mut j = 0
while j < take
  match list.max(remaining)
  when Some(m)
    match list.index_of(remaining, m)
    when Some(idx)
      remaining = remove_at(remaining, idx)
    when None
      ()
    end
    if j == 0
      output = "#{m}"
    else
      output = "#{output} #{m}"
    end
  when None
    ()
  end
  j = j + 1
end

println(output)
